title: neobreonia decaryana
side_effects: Neobreonia decaryana can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to liver damage and kidney problems. Additionally, improper use of the plant has been linked to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, hives, and respiratory issues. In severe cases, misuse of Neobreonia decaryana can result in cardiovascular complications, including rapid heart rate and blood pressure fluctuations.
